business.headline: Donald Trump made 3,700 stock trades in just 90 days. Why Wall Street is shockedFinancial disclosures linked to US President Donald Trump reveal more than 3,700 stock trades in just three months, triggering alarm on Wall Street over the scale, timing and potential conflicts involved.

Sensex down 900 points: Why is the stock market falling today?The benchmark BSE Sensex plunged 939.10 points, or 1.25%, to 74,298.89 at 9:37 am, while the NSE Nifty50 fell 284 points, or 1.20%, to 23,359.50.

ICICI Prudential Life shares down 7% today: Why the stock is falling sharplyICICI Prudential Life shares dropped Rs 39.70, or 7.41%, to Rs 495.90 on the National Stock Exchange of India as of 9:32 am, making it one of the top losers on the benchmark indices.

Sensex opens over 800 points lower, Nifty below 23,400; Tata Steel down 4%The S&P BSE Sensex fell 807.71 points to 74,430.28 at 9:18 am, while the Nifty 50 slipped 249.75 points to 23,393.75. Most of the broader market indices also slipped.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock market rise or fall today?Markets are set for a weak open after a drone attack on a UAE nuclear plant pushed oil prices higher. Rising crude, a record-low rupee and mixed institutional flows are keeping sentiment cautious.

Will petrol and diesel prices rise again after Russian oil waiver ends?India may face fresh pressure on petrol and diesel prices after the US let a waiver on Russian oil purchases expire. With crude above USD 100 and West Asia tensions high, refiners and consumers could come under added strain.

Gautam Adani launches Rs 150 crore rural eye care initiative in BiharTogether, the Adani Centre for Eye (ACE) and Adani Training in Ophthalmic Medicine (ATOM) will create an ecosystem that will be capable of performing 3.3 lakh eye surgeries annually and training 1,000 eye care professionals every year.

The fine print behind unlimited health insurance policiesHandcrafted ByOur Editors In the first part of our series "The Unlimited Illusion", we explain how unlimited health insurance policies work, what insurers mean by "unlimited", and why the fine print matters.

CNG prices increased in Delhi-NCR by Re 1, second hike in 2 daysCNG will now cost Rs 80.09 per kg in Delhi, while consumers in Noida and Ghaziabad will pay Rs 88.70 per kg. The latest increase comes just two days after CNG prices were raised by Rs 2 per kg on Friday, resulting in an overall hike of Rs 3 per kg.

Delhi govt cuts VAT on jet fuel; move to benefit airlines, passengersDelhi has cut VAT on aviation turbine fuel from 25 per cent to 7 per cent after Maharashtra made a similar move. The step is aimed at easing pressure on airlines and passengers as global jet fuel prices climb.

Govt restricts silver bar imports, 99.9% purity category now needs permitThe Centre has restricted imports of certain silver bars, including 99.9% purity silver, with immediate effect amid the ongoing West Asia conflict.

Selective compliance, systemic exclusion: How workers are left outIn the seventh part of this series on labour laws and Inspector Raj, a factory inspection from 1988 reveals how workers, though recorded for contributions, remained excluded from statutory benefits through selective and incomplete compliance.

No Claim Bonus: The motor insurance reward many drivers lose unknowinglyHidden inside every motor insurance policy is something called No Claim Bonus, or NCB, a reward insurers give customers for not making claims. Over time, it can reduce premiums significantly. Yet despite being one of the most valuable benefits in motor insurance, it is also one of the least understood.

Inflation finds new fuelHandcrafted ByOur Editors India's latest fuel price hike could trigger a wider inflation ripple across the economy, raising concerns over costlier food, deliveries, transport and growing pressure on household budgets.

Govt hikes export duty on petrol to Rs 3 per litre, cuts diesel, ATF levies from May 16The differential taxation strategy is expected to boost government revenue from petroleum exports without affecting price stability for Indian consumers.

Fuel price hike right move, oil firms can't absorb loss for long period: Ashok LahiriIn an exclusive interview with India Today, Dr Ashok Lahiri, the newly appointed Vice-Chairman of NITI Aayog, discussed the recent fuel price hikes, the state of the Indian economy, and the impact of the West Asia conflict.
Sensex, Nifty slip as rupee hits record lowThe benchmark BSE Sensex fell 160.73 points, or 0.21%, to close at 75,237.99, while the NSE Nifty50 slipped 46.10 points, or 0.19%, to settle at 23,643.50.

Reliance-Disney's JioStar sues Zee over alleged unauthorised film broadcasts: ReportJioStar has moved against Zee over alleged unauthorised telecasts of 12 Bollywood films. The clash adds to a widening rights battle between two major media companies.

Maharashtra cuts VAT on jet fuel from 18% to 7% amid rising aviation costsFuel is one of the biggest expenses for airlines and accounts for nearly 35-40% of an airline's operating costs. The tax reduction is expected to lower refuelling costs at Mumbai airport, which is India's second-busiest airport after Delhi and handles around 15% of the country's air traffic.

Petrol, diesel prices could rise over next 3-4 months amid crude oil crisisThe Rs 3-per-litre fuel price hike may not be the last, with analysts warning that persistently high crude oil prices could trigger more petrol and diesel price increases over coming three to four months.

entertainment.headline: Aamir Khan says Thugs of Hindostan was 'basically Sholay', admits he made an error Aamir Khan said Thugs of Hindostan followed a Sholay-like format and changed heavily during casting. He said prioritising his character Firangi over the story was a mistake that hurt the film.

Such a shame: Ameesha Patel calls out online hate after Alia's Cannes appearance Actor Ameesha Patel posted a sharp message on social media after Alia Bhatt was trolled over her Cannes appearance. The actor wrote about the shift in attention to pattern of online negativity aimed at Indian actors.

Vir Das calls out flyer going barefoot mid-meal, internet takes sides Vir Das sparked an online debate after calling out a flyer for going barefoot and scratching his feet during a flight meal service. While many backed the comedian over hygiene concerns, others felt such incidents should be handled privately instead of online.

Soni Razdan reacts as trolls claim daughter Alia was ignored at Cannes Soni Razdan reacted after a viral Cannes clip of Alia Bhatt triggered online trolling. The row widened as a writer called the backlash a misread, gendered response driven by validation anxieties in a viral post on Instagram.

Pati Patni Aur...box office Day 3: Comedy-drama wraps 1st weekend with Rs 24 crore Pati Patni Aur Woh Do posed a stronger Sunday at the box office after a steady opening weekend. The film earned Rs 21 crore over the first weekend.

Suriya's Karuppu marks his box office comeback with Rs 120 crore in 3 days Suriya's Karuppu has opened strongly, grossing Rs 120.75 crore worldwide in three days. The steady rise in collections and mixed reactions has sharpened focus on his box-office return.

Cannes cameras did not snub Alia Bhatt. The Indian internet did Alia Bhatt's Cannes red carpet appearance this year triggered online claims that photographers ignored her. The reaction shifted attention from the event to a wider fixation on foreign validation and Indian celebrity scrutiny.

RJ Mahvash addresses unfollow buzz with Yuzvendra Chahal, calls him a friend RJ Mahvash has addressed ongoing speculation about her relationship with Yuzvendra Chahal, clarifying that they have always shared a friendship. She also dismissed social media unfollow rumours, saying there is no major issue between them.

Malavika Mohanan calls media's Vijay-Trisha question distasteful, urges respect Actor Malavika Mohanan objected after being asked an uncomfortable question about Vijay and Trisha at a media event in Chennai. She later said some questions were distasteful and urged more respectful media interactions.

Keep growing: Sara Ali Khan thanks darshaks for love on Pati Patni Aur Woh Do Sara Ali Khan thanked fans for the love and support for Pati Patni Aur Woh Do. She also urged audiences to keep supporting the film as it shows steady box office growth.

BTS from Irrfan's unreleased directorial Alvida featuring Nawazuddin goes viral Irrfan's wife Sutapa Sikdar has shared an emotional behind-the-scenes video from his unreleased directorial Alvida. The clip features Nawazuddin Siddiqui and offers a rare glimpse into Irrfan's work as a filmmaker.

Viral video: Amitabh Bachchan shares glimpse of Qajar-era Vishnu temple in Iran Amitabh Bachchan's latest Instagram post featuring a historic Vishnu temple in Iran has gone viral online, drawing attention to a fascinating cultural link between India and Iran.

Alone and Lonely: Salman Khan shows off ripped abs, cryptic caption sparks buzz Salman Khan shared a new Instagram photo showing his toned abs while reclining on a sofa. Its caption dissecting the notions of "alone" and "lonely" made the post a talking point.

Cannes 2026: Restored Malayalam classic Amma Ariyan draws standing ovation Malayalam classic Amma Ariyan has returned to the global spotlight after its restored 4K version received a standing ovation at Cannes Film Festival. The screening has sparked renewed interest in the iconic film.

RJ Mahvash confirms India's Got Latent Season 2 appearance after Samay's invite RJ Mahvash has confirmed accepting Samay Raina's invitation to appear on India's Got Latent Season 2. She also opened up about standing by fellow creators amid controversies.

Tamil film producer K Rajan dies by suicide at 85 in Chennai Tamil film producer K Rajan died by suicide on Sunday in Chennai. His death has shocked the industry. The police are investigating the case and details are awaited. Rajan is known for films like Doubles, Aval Paavam and Ninaikatha Naaliai.

Aarti Ravi's mother says grandson faced stress during actor Ravi Mohan dispute Aarti Ravi's mother Sujatha Vijaykumar has publicly answered Ravi Mohan's allegations and said the family held back because her grandson is under stress during his Class 10 examinations. Her remarks also reject claims about medical payments, defend support for Aarti, and point to possible cybercrime action.

Are Sreeleela and cricketer Tilak Varma in a relationship? Here's what we know Speculation continues to circulate online around a possible link between Sreeleela and cricketer Tilak Varma. A source close to the matter has responded to the rumours, addressing the ongoing buzz around their alleged relationship.

Actor Vishal questions CM Vijay over giving film portfolio to Egmore MLA Rajmohan Actor Vishal questioned Chief Minister Vijay over assigning the film technology and cinematograph portfolio to Egmore MLA Rajmohan. His remarks have now triggered reactions within the film industry and political circles.

general news.headline: Congress's VD Satheesan takes oath as Kerala CM as UDF returns to power Live UpdatesEnding a decade in opposition, the Congress-led UDF returned to power in Kerala with VD Satheesan taking oath as the Chief Minister today.

Donald Trump made 3,700 stock trades in just 90 days. Why Wall Street is shockedFinancial disclosures linked to US President Donald Trump reveal more than 3,700 stock trades in just three months, triggering alarm on Wall Street over the scale, timing and potential conflicts involved.

Accused set free, a dangerous precedent: Father of woman found dead in BhopalTwisha Sharma's family protested outside Chief Minister Mohan Yadav's residence on Sunday. Her father said that he will set himself on fire if justice was not delivered.

Lack of understanding: India rejects Dutch concerns over press freedom, minoritiesAddressing the media at The Hague, Ministry of External Affairs Secretary (West) Sibi George said criticism of India often stemmed from inadequate knowledge about the country's social and democratic fabric.

Lost Kerala but not hope: How Left's umbilical cord remains intactThe Congress-led UDF has taken over the reins of Kerala, ending Pinarayi Vijayan-led LDF's 10-year rule and the Communist bloc's last remaining government in India. But here is why a Congress-led UDF victory in Kerala would not mean that the Left has been wiped out forever in the state.

I'm trapped, can't talk: Noida woman, found dead in Bhopal, sent this last textTwisha Sharma's family has intensified its protest over her mysterious death in Bhopal, demanding a fresh AIIMS Delhi post-mortem and refusing last rites, while her final "I am trapped" message to a friend has added a new layer of intrigue to the case.

8-hour CBI raid at house of man who bought leaked NEET paper for daughterDuring the raid, officials examined documents and questioned family members while tracing when the paper was obtained, who supplied it, and how the alleged Rs 5 lakh deal was arranged.

Pilot was clutching controls: Witness reveals morgue horror after Air India crashRomin Vohra, who lost three family members in the Air India crash last June, told the Daily Mail that he witnessed scenes inside the hospital morgue that he "can never unsee".

Worked hard for 30 years: Indian eatery owner ordered to leave Japan, breaks downAn Indian restaurant owner in Japan has been ordered to leave the country after authorities rejected his business manager visa renewal, forcing him to shut down a restaurant he has operated for 18 years.

Live: After VD Satheesan's swearing-in, cabinet members take oathVD Satheesan Oath Ceremony Live Updates: Congress leader VD Satheesan was sworn in as Kerala's next Chief Minister, ushering in a United Democratic Front (UDF) government after a 10-year hiatus. The alliance stormed to power with 102 seats in the 140-member Assembly, ending the CPI(M)-led Left rule. Along with the chief minister, his 20-member cabinet is also set to be sworn in today at 10 pm at a grand event in Thiruvananthapuram. Senior Congress leaders, prominent figures from various walks of life, and chief ministers of opposition-ruled states are likely to attend the ceremony. Stay tuned to IndiaToday.in for all the latest updates.

Leaked Pakistani cable exposes US role in ex-PM Imran Khan's ousterA leaked Pakistani diplomatic cable has reignited controversy over the ouster of former Prime Minister Imran Khan, with investigative outlet Drop Site publishing the original document at the centre of the cricketer-turned-politician's claims of US-backed regime change.

Vijay skips Kerala CM swearing-in amid security, crowd management concernsSources said Vijay had no plans to visit Kerala for the ceremony despite being invited earlier, with officials citing administrative commitments and fears that his presence could trigger massive crowd surges and create logistical challenges for law enforcement agencies.

Cannes cameras did not snub Alia Bhatt. The Indian internet didAlia Bhatt's Cannes red carpet appearance this year triggered online claims that photographers ignored her. The reaction shifted attention from the event to a wider fixation on foreign validation and Indian celebrity scrutiny.

Top divers race to recover Maldives tourists' bodies from shark-infested watersAn elite rescue team has reached the Maldives after a military diver died while trying to recover four bodies trapped 200 feet underwater. The divers' association warned that the bodies must be retrieved quickly before sharks and harsh underwater conditions further complicate the mission.

Birth rates are falling globally, your smartphone may be the reason behind itAcross the world, countries are witnessing a sharp decline in fertility rate. Researchers are now examining whether smartphones and social media are contributing to falling birth rates worldwide.

Will petrol and diesel prices rise again after Russian oil waiver ends?India may face fresh pressure on petrol and diesel prices after the US let a waiver on Russian oil purchases expire. With crude above USD 100 and West Asia tensions high, refiners and consumers could come under added strain.

Indian woman in Bangkok says fresh air, safety matter more than 10-minute deliveryShreya Mahendru said her move to Bangkok was shaped by a search for cleaner, quieter living. Her post sparked a wider debate on liveability, civic conditions and the value of everyday peace.

Virgin girl's silence is consent: Taliban legalise child marriage in AfghanistanThe Taliban has published a new family law in Afghanistan covering marriage, divorce and child marriage. Rights advocates say the rules deepen concerns over consent and women's autonomy under Taliban rule.

From NEET paper leak to CBSE row: Rahul Gandhi calls Education Ministry a 'disaster'Leader of Opposition Rahul Gandhi on Sunday accused the Centre of failing students across age groups, linking the NEET-UG paper leak, CBSE assessment concerns and the new Class 9 language policy. His remarks come amid the expanding NEET probe and growing scrutiny over the education system.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi rewrites IPL record books with six-sitting featIPL 2026: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi hit his 43rd six of the season during Rajasthan Royals' match against Delhi Capitals. At 15, he has reset the Indian benchmark and moved within sight of Chris Gayle's overall IPL record.

How does a UPI QR code work? It is mathematics hiding in plain sightEvery UPI payment you make by scanning a QR code triggers a cascade of sophisticated mathematics in under a second. Here is the science of why that crumpled, faded square on a shop counter still processes your payment perfectly every single time.

CM-elect announces Kerala Cabinet, 21 ministers to take oath today Live UpdatesVD Satheesan unveiled the 21-member Cabinet and said the full UDF ministry would take oath at 10 am on Monday. He said the list balanced social and regional representation, with portfolio talks almost complete.

North India will be on fire today; South, East and West to witness rainThe IMD has issued heat wave warnings for Delhi, Punjab, Haryana and Rajasthan on May 18, with temperatures expected to touch 45 degrees Celsius in the capital.

2 US fighter jets collide, explode during Idaho air show, crew eject safelyVideos shared online showed the two aircraft colliding in the air before breaking apart. Moments later, four parachutes could be seen descending toward the ground.

sports.headline: Preity Zinta's intense chat with Shreyas Iyer goes viral as PBKS lose six in a row Preity Zinta spoke at length with Shreyas Iyer after Punjab Kings lost to Royal Challengers Bengaluru in Dharamsala. The exchange drew attention as Punjab slumped to a sixth straight defeat and RCB sealed a playoff place.

Who is Aaron Rai? An Indian-origin golfer gives Britain its first PGA title in over 100 years Aaron Rai, an Indian-origin golfer, surged past Alex Smalley and Jon Rahm to win the PGA Championship, becoming the first Englishman in 107 years to lift the title.

Virat Kohli deserves Glenn Phillips-level hype for athleticism, say fans after viral video Livewire at 37, Virat Kohli left fans in awe during RCB's win over PBKS in Dharamshala. A viral clip of his electric fielding sparked massive social media reactions, with fans hailing the icon as the greatest athlete India has ever produced.

CSK vs SRH: Dhoni's potential farewell masks playoff drama in Chennai Chennai Super Kings face SunRisers Hyderabad in their final home game of the season. While a playoff spot might be at stake, the entire attention is set to be on the possibility that MS Dhoni might announce his retirement after the game.

Kotla feels like an away ground: Badani's brutal verdict on DC's home disadvantage Delhi Capitals coach Hemang Badani delivered a blunt assessment of his side's home struggles despite DC's crucial five-wicket win, admitting the Arun Jaitley Stadium no longer feels like a home venue after the team managed just two wins in seven matches in Delhi this IPL season.

IPL Play of the Day: Mitchell Starc comes clutch to rip the life out of RR IPL 2026, DC vs RR: Mitchell Starc rattled Rajasthan Royals with a sensational over in which he picked up three wickets, laying the foundation for Delhi Capitals' five-wicket win on Sunday at the Arun Jaitley Stadium in New Delhi.

IPL chairman Arun Dhumal interview: Leagues over bilaterals, real life over reels In an exclusive conversation at the HPCA Stadium in Dharamsala, league chairman Arun Singh Dhumal is candid about the IPL's billion-dollar future, the mid-season discipline storm, and why he thinks social media is the most anti-social thing ever invented.

Elina Svitolina wins Italian Open, Gael Monfils bursts with pride: What a woman Elina Svitolina defeated Coco Gauff to win the 2026 Italian Open title in Rome, completing an emotional comeback story as Gael Monfils paid a heartfelt tribute to his wife. The Ukrainian secured her first WTA 1000 title since giving birth to her daughter in 2022.

Jose Mourinho breaks silence on Real Madrid rumours Benfica head coach Jose Mourinho has clarified rumours of him joining Real Madrid in the upcoming season. Mourinho has said that there has been no direct approach made to him by the club in the recent weeks.

Riyan Parag furious after RR's loss to DC: Shouldn't be in race for IPL playoffs Rajasthan Royals captain Riyan Parag was not happy with the team after sloppy mistakes in the field led to the team's loss against Delhi Capitals. Parag said that if those are the team's standards, they do not deserve to be in contention for the playoff spots.

Delhi Capitals do PBKS a favour with commanding win over RajasthanIPL 2026: Delhi Capitals outclassed Rajasthan Royals by 5-wicket at the Arun Jaitley Stadium to keep their slim play-off hopes alive. Mitchell Starc was the star performer for the Capitals with a fiery spell that dismantled RR's middle order.

MotoGP: Horrible crash in Spain sends Alex Marquez into the wallA horrifying high-speed crash involving Alex Marquez brought the Catalunya Grand Prix to a halt on Sunday after the Spanish rider slammed into Pedro Acosta and crashed violently into the barriers. Marquez suffered multiple injuries, while his motorcycle was left completely destroyed in the incident.

Jannik Sinner clinches Italian Open title, becomes youngest to Career Golden MastersJannik Sinner defeated Casper Ruud 6-4, 6-4 to win the 2026 Italian Open, becoming the youngest player to complete the Career Golden Masters by winning all nine ATP Masters titles. The victory also saw him make history as the first man to win the first five Masters events in a single season.

Rishabh Pant likely to lose leadership role in Test team amid IPL failuresTwo terrible seasons with Lucknow Super Giants has put questions on Rishabh Pant's leadership credentials. Rumours suggest that BCCI top brass are considering removing him from the vice-captain's position in Test cricket.

Could IPL playoffs be played behind closed doors amid PM Modi's appeal?IPL 2026: Concerns have emerged over the scheduling and logistics of a major sporting event in India amid ongoing geopolitical tensions and calls for austerity measures. Organisers said they will follow any directives issued by the Government of India regarding potential changes.

Farewell on IPL return? MS Dhoni trains before CSK vs SRH, sparks buzzIPL 2026: MS Dhoni is set to be at the centre of massive anticipation as CSK prepare for their final home game of IPL 2026 against SRH at Chepauk, with speculation growing over a possible appearance after a calf injury ruled him out of all matches so far.

Bruno Fernandes equals Premier League record as Man Utd win VAR-marred clashBruno Fernandes matched the Premier League assist record as Manchester United beat Nottingham Forest 3-2 at Old Trafford. The win kept United third, but a prolonged VAR handball call dominated the fallout.

Punjab's IPL season did not derail due to off-field controversies: Bowling coachPunjab Kings are on the brink of being eliminated from the IPL 2026 playoffs race. Punjab have endured a difficult time on and off the field, resulting in a horror performance in the second half this season.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi rewrites IPL record books with six-sitting featIPL 2026: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi hit his 43rd six of the season during Rajasthan Royals' match against Delhi Capitals. At 15, he has reset the Indian benchmark and moved within sight of Chris Gayle's overall IPL record.

Punjab Kings in freefall after 6 defeats in a row. Can they still reach playoffs?IPL 2026, PBKS vs RCB: Shreyas Iyer's Punjab Kings lost to Royal Challengers Bengaluru on Sunday, May 17 at the HPCA Stadium in Dharamsala and are now in serious trouble as far as qualifying for the playoffs is concerned.

RCB storm into playoffs as Punjab Kings complete beyond-believable fall in HimachalIPL 2026, PBKS vs RCB: Punjab Kings suffered their sixth straight defeat as the Himachal escape offered them no relief. Fifties from Virat Kohli and Venkatesh Iyer propelled RCB into the playoffs after the tourists sealed a 23-run win in Dharamsala on Sunday.

Grown men reduced to tears after Hearts break on final-day of Scottish leagueHearts lost the Scottish Premiership title 3-1 to Celtic after a late collapse at Parkhead. The defeat ended a 226-day stay on top and preserved the old order in Scottish football.

Bangladesh tighten grip in Sylhet Test, Pakistan struggle despite Babar Azam resistanceBAN vs PAK, 2nd Test: Bangladesh have taken a lead of 156 runs in Sylhet after Mahmudul Hasan Joy scored a sparkling century. Pakistan had conceded a lead of 46 runs despite Babar Azam's quickfire 68.

Did KKR field injured Varun Chakravarthy vs GT to keep playoff hopes alive?IPL 2026: Varun Chakravarthy's suspected foot fracture has put Kolkata Knight Riders and the BCCI in direct focus. The episode has sharpened questions over franchise workload, medical oversight and India's T20 plans.

Ravi Shastri urges BCCI to fast-track Vaibhav Sooryavanshi into India setupRavi Shastri has urged India's selectors to move quickly on Vaibhav Sooryavanshi after the teenager's IPL surge. With an India A call-up already secured, the Ireland route now looks like a credible fast-track.

500 and counting: Virat Kohli's consistency in unmatched, extends his IPL recordPBKS vs RCB: Virat Kohli has registered another 500-run IPL season, extending his record. The feat highlights his sustained consistency in the tournament.

Why is RCB captain Rajat Patidar not playing against PBKS in Dharamshala?IPL 2026: Rajat Patidar did not take the field for Royal Challengers Bengaluru against Punjab Kings in Dharamshala, with Jitesh Sharma stepping in for the toss and Suash Sharma replacing himas an impact substitute.

Sunil Narine, the greatest player in IPL history: Ambati Rayudu makes bold claimAmbati Rayudu called Sunil Narine the greatest player in IPL history after KKR's win over Gujarat Titans. Sanjay Bangar backed the view as Narine marked his 200th match with another decisive spell.

Xabi Alonso announced as new Chelsea manager, to take charge from July 1Chelsea have appointed Xabi Alonso as manager on a four-year contract from July 1, 2026. The move comes after FA Cup final pain and a season that leaves them outside Europe.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
